Title: Wheat TaTIP4;1 Confers Enhanced Tolerance to Drought, Salt and Osmotic Stress in Arabidopsis and Rice

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: TaTIP4;1 is one of eleven TIPs identified in wheat and belongs to a TIP4 subgroup. Its expression pattern suggests functional importance in leaves under dehydration (PEG) and in roots under salt and ABA signaling. The study emphasizes that TIP aquaporins, including TaTIP4;1, have a generally positive role in plant responses to drought, salinity and osmotic stress by facilitating water movement across the tonoplast.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. TaTIP4;1 expression in wheat is strongly induced by PEG6000, NaCl, ABA and H2O2 in a tissue- and treatment-dependent manner, with high basal expression in roots, stems and leaves.
2. Overexpression of TaTIP4;1 in Arabidopsis and rice does not alter growth under normal conditions but significantly improves drought, salt and osmotic stress tolerance, as shown by higher survival, seed germination rates, root elongation, and plant height, and reduced leaf water loss.
3. Under salinity, TaTIP4;1 overexpression markedly suppresses Na+ accumulation and lowers Na+/K+ ratios in Arabidopsis and rice, contributing to better ion homeostasis.
4. TaTIP4;1 overexpression alleviates oxidative damage by reducing H2O2 and MDA accumulation and by enhancing CAT (and in rice, SOD) activities, and modulates proline levels in both Arabidopsis and rice under drought and salt/osmotic stress.
5. In TaTIP4;1-overexpressing Arabidopsis and rice, multiple drought-responsive genes (e.g., AtDREB1B, AtRD29A/B, OsDREB2, OsNAC1, OsPIP2;1, OsRAB16C, OsPOX1) and salt-responsive genes (e.g., AtSOS2, AtNHX1, AtRD29A/B, AtPKS5, AtCAT1, OsPIP2;1, OsP5CS, OsCATB) show stronger stress-induced transcription than in WT, indicating a positive role of TaTIP4;1 in activating stress-response gene expression.
6. In yeast, expression of TaTIP4;1 increases tolerance to NaCl and sorbitol, whereas mutation of individual serines S83, S201 or S207 to alanine reduces this tolerance, suggesting phosphorylation-dependent regulation of TaTIP4;1 activity.
7. Overall, TaTIP4;1 functions as an essential positive regulator of seed germination and seedling growth under drought, salt and osmotic stress by affecting water relations, ROS and ion homeostasis, proline accumulation and the expression of numerous stress-responsive genes, making it a strong candidate gene for engineering crop tolerance to water deficit and salinity.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TaTIP4;1 acts as a positive regulator of plant tolerance to drought, salt and osmotic stress: its overexpression in Arabidopsis and rice enhances seed germination and seedling growth under these stresses, increases survival and water content while reducing leaf water loss, Na+ accumulation, Na+/K+ ratio, ROS and lipid peroxidation, and promoting antioxidant enzyme activity and proline accumulation; in yeast, TaTIP4;1 expression also enhances tolerance to salt and osmotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: TaTIP4;1 is a tonoplast aquaporin whose activity is modulated by phosphorylation of cytosolic serine residues: in yeast, mutation of serines S83, S201 or S207 to alanine reduces the TaTIP4;1-mediated enhancement of salt and sorbitol tolerance, indicating phosphorylation-dependent regulation. In planta, TaTIP4;1 overexpression alters cellular water relations, represses Na+ accumulation and Na+/K+ ratio, reduces ROS and membrane lipid peroxidation, enhances CAT (and partly SOD) activity, and modifies proline accumulation, which together help maintain water and redox homeostasis and ion balance under drought, salt and osmotic stress. TaTIP4;1 overexpression also positively affects the transcript levels of many drought and salt responsive genes under stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in ABA- and stress-responsive regulatory networks: TaTIP4;1 expression in wheat is induced by ABA, PEG and NaCl, and its overexpression in Arabidopsis and rice enhances expression of multiple drought and salt responsive genes involved in ABA signaling, ion transport, water transport and ROS detoxification under dehydration and salinity.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: TaTIP4;1 --(activation of stress-induced expression)--> AtDREB1B, AtRD29A, AtRD29B (dehydration leads to greater induction in TaTIP4;1 OE Arabidopsis than in WT); TaTIP4;1 --(activation)--> OsDREB2, OsNAC1, OsPIP2;1, OsRAB16C, OsPOX1 (water deficit causes stronger induction in TaTIP4;1 OE rice than in WT); TaTIP4;1 --(activation)--> AtSOS2, AtNHX1, AtRD29A, AtRD29B, AtPKS5, AtCAT1 (NaCl results in higher transcript levels in TaTIP4;1 OE Arabidopsis than in WT); TaTIP4;1 --(activation)--> OsPIP2;1, OsP5CS, OsCATB (NaCl induces higher expression in TaTIP4;1 OE rice than in WT).
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Tonoplast intrinsic protein (TIP) aquaporin, six-transmembrane membrane protein
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In wheat, TaTIP4;1 transcripts are strongly induced by 10% PEG6000 (drought/osmotic) in leaves (6–24 h) and roots (6–9 h), by 200 mM NaCl in leaves (all time points) and roots (3–24 h), by 100 μM ABA in roots (6–9 h), and by 10 mM H2O2 in leaves (3–12 h) and roots (6–9 h). Tissue expression analysis shows strong expression in roots, stems and leaves. In Arabidopsis and rice transgenic lines, TaTIP4;1 is highly expressed in selected overexpression lines relative to controls.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PEG6000-induced water deficit/osmotic stress; NaCl-induced salinity; mannitol-induced osmotic stress; ABA and H2O2 treatments in wheat seedlings; sorbitol and NaCl stress in yeast.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought, salinity, osmotic stress), seed germination, root and seedling growth, ROS homeostasis, ion (Na+/K+) homeostasis
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought tolerance, salt tolerance, osmotic stress tolerance); seed germination rate; primary root growth; seedling vigor; ion homeostasis; oxidative stress response
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In rice TaTIP4;1-overexpressing lines, plant heights under stress were >1.7-fold (5% PEG6000), 2.0-fold (10% PEG6000), 1.5-fold (50 mM NaCl) and 1.8-fold (75 mM NaCl) of WT values. Survival rates of OE1, OE2 and OE3 after 10 d at 200 mM NaCl were 3.6, 3.3 and 3.5 times those of WT, and after 10 d at 200 mM mannitol were 2.5, 2.3 and 2.9 times those of WT. In Arabidopsis, TaTIP4;1 OE lines showed significantly higher survival rates and relative leaf water contents, and significantly lower leaf water-loss rates, than WT and VC under drought and salt treatments, and significantly reduced Na+ contents and Na+/K+ ratios under salt stress.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Under normal (non-stress) conditions, Arabidopsis and rice overexpression lines show growth and seed germination similar to wild type, indicating little or no penalty on basal growth from TaTIP4;1 overexpression.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Wheat (Triticum aestivum L. cv. Chinese Spring) seedlings; Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Col-0 WT and TaTIP4;1 overexpression (OE6, OE9, OE10) and vector control (VC) lines; rice (Oryza sativa L. ssp. japonica, cv. Nipponbare) WT and TaTIP4;1 overexpression lines (OE1, OE2, OE3); yeast Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain INVSC1 expressing wild-type or serine-mutated TaTIP4;1.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qRT-PCR analysis of TaTIP4;1 expression in wheat under PEG6000, NaCl, ABA and H2O2 treatments and in different tissues; Agrobacterium-mediated transformation to generate Arabidopsis (pBI121) and rice (pCAMBIA1300) TaTIP4;1 overexpression lines; drought assays in soil by withholding water and rewatering, and salt stress in soil by watering with 250 mM NaCl; in vitro stress assays using 1/2 MS medium supplemented with defined concentrations of NaCl or mannitol; PEG6000, NaCl and mannitol stress treatments in liquid culture for rice; measurements of survival rate, seed germination rate, primary root length, plant height, relative water content, and leaf water-loss rate; biochemical assays of Na+ and K+ contents, H2O2 and MDA levels, CAT, POD and SOD activities, and proline contents using commercial kits; qRT-PCR of multiple drought- and salt-responsive genes in Arabidopsis and rice under PEG6000 and NaCl treatments; yeast functional assays using pYES2-driven TaTIP4;1 or serine-to-alanine mutants in INVSC1 under NaCl and sorbitol stress.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: TaTIP4;1 coding sequence (768 bp CDS) was amplified from wheat (Triticum aestivum cv. Chinese Spring) cDNA, cloned into binary vectors (pBI121 for Arabidopsis, pCAMBIA1300 for rice) and into yeast expression vector pYES2, and its stress-inducible expression in wheat was characterized by qRT-PCR under PEG, NaCl, ABA and H2O2 treatments.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Overexpression of TaTIP4;1 markedly enhances tolerance to drought, salt and osmotic stress in Arabidopsis and rice at seed germination and seedling stages, while not affecting growth under normal conditions. TaTIP4;1 improves plant water status, reduces Na+ accumulation and Na+/K+ ratio, alleviates ROS and lipid peroxidation damage, and promotes proline accumulation and the expression of multiple stress-responsive genes. These features indicate TaTIP4;1 is a promising genetic engineering target to improve crop performance (including cereals) under water deficit and salinity, by enhancing seed germination, root growth and early seedling vigor.
